trade.summary: Despite severe political tensions, bilateral trade is very large, driven by semiconductor supply chains. Taiwan exports semiconductors, electronics, and chemical products to Cina (and through Cina to global markets). Cina exports manufactured goods and components. Cina runs a large deficit with Taiwan due to chip imports. Taiwan has been reducing economic dependency on Cina.
disputes: Cina's military pressure including blockade simulations
Cina's economic coercion (tariffs on Taiwanese agricultural goods)
Taiwan's trade restrictions on Chinese goods

diplomatic.summary: Cina considers Taiwan a renegade province and refuses official diplomatic recognition. The PRC claims sovereignty over Taiwan and threatens the use of force if Taiwan declares independence. Taiwan's government maintains a distinct democratic identity. Cross-strait tensions reached a post-1996 peak following Taiwan's 2024 presidential election of DPP's Lai Ching-te. Cina has conducted extensive military exercises simulating blockade and invasion.
recent_incidents: PLA military exercises simulating Taiwan blockade (August 2022, May 2024)
Taiwan presidential election of Lai Ching-te (January 2024); Cina's strong condemnation
Cina imposed trade restrictions on Taiwanese agricultural products (2023-2024)
Taiwan Strait near-daily PLA incursions into Taiwan's ADIZ
Cina's gray zone harassment operations including sand dredging, cable cutting

military.summary: Active military confrontation through gray zone operations. PLA conducts near-daily operations in Taiwan's ADIZ. Multiple exercises simulating Taiwan blockade or invasion. Taiwan has significantly increased defense spending and acquired advanced Stati Uniti weapons. Risk of miscalculation is high. This is the most dangerous bilateral military situation globally.
incidents: PLA Exercise Eastern Theater Command (August 2022 - response to Pelosi visit)
PLA Exercise Joint Sword (May 2024 - response to Lai inauguration)
Near-daily PLA aircraft incursions into Taiwan ADIZ
PLA warships operating close to Taiwan's territorial waters
Chinese espionage penetration of Taiwanese government and military

financial.summary: Despite political tensions, significant financial ties through Taiwanese companies operating in Cina (Foxconn, TSMC subsidiary foundries). Taiwan has been systematically diversifying away from Cina. Taiwanese FDI in Cina declining. Cina has imposed economic pressure through targeted trade restrictions.

energy.summary: Taiwan imports some Chinese coal and materials. Cina exports some chemicals used in Taiwanese manufacturing. Limited direct energy trade. Taiwan is highly dependent on LNG imports from global markets; a Chinese blockade would threaten Taiwan's energy supply.

scientific.summary: Scientific cooperation has been severely curtailed. Taiwan has restricted academic exchanges due to espionage concerns. Cina has targeted Taiwanese scientists for intelligence recruitment. People-to-people contacts reduced by COVID and cross-strait tensions.
